skill.name: nx-workspace-patterns
skill.description: Configure Nx monorepo workspaces with project boundaries, cache settings, affected CI workflows, and generator patterns.
skill.summary: Apply practical Nx workspace patterns for boundaries, caching, CI, and generators.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 25 command, 3 URL, 4 filesystem, and 2 weak-crypto patterns. AI review found these to be false positives from Markdown examples, Nx configuration, resource links, and unrelated text. One low-risk content issue remains because cache token placeholders may encourage committed secrets.

low_findings.title: Cache Token Placeholders in Configuration Example
low_findings.description: The remote caching example shows accessToken and nxCloudAccessToken placeholder fields in nx.json. This is not malicious, but users may copy real tokens into versioned configuration instead of secret storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show token placeholder fields in a configuration example. Confidence is below very high because no real secret value or exfiltration behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Large Nx workspaces are hard to structure, cache, and test efficiently. This skill provides reusable patterns for boundaries, generators, CI, and caching.
actual_capabilities: Designs Nx workspace layouts for apps, libraries, and tooling.
Defines library tags and module boundary rules.
Creates target defaults and named inputs for cacheable tasks.
Plans affected command workflows for CI pipelines.
Adapts generator and project configuration templates.
limitations: Does not inspect live repositories unless the agent reads project files.
Does not execute Nx commands by itself.
Requires adjustment for each Nx version and plugin set.
Does not replace security review of CI secrets.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New Nx Workspace
use_cases.description: Create a clean workspace structure with apps, shared libraries, tags, and cacheable targets.

use_cases.title: Improve Monorepo CI Time
use_cases.description: Use affected commands and caching patterns to reduce unnecessary lint, test, build, and e2e jobs.

use_cases.title: Enforce Project Boundaries
use_cases.description: Define dependency rules that keep features, UI, data access, and utility libraries separated.

prompt_templates.title: Create Workspace Layout
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new monorepo
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Nx workspace layout for my apps and libraries. Include tags, library types, and the main configuration files.

prompt_templates.title: Add Module Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Nx project tags and propose module boundary rules for apps, features, UI, data access, and utilities.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize CI With Affected Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Nx CI workflow that runs affected lint, test, build, and e2e tasks with caching and safe parallelism.

prompt_templates.title: Design Generator Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a custom Nx generator for feature libraries. Include naming rules, generated files, tags, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: I need a workspace structure for a web app, API, and shared UI library.
output_examples.output: A proposed apps and libs layout, recommended tags, boundary rules, and cacheable targets for build, lint, test, and e2e.

output_examples.input: Our CI runs every test on each pull request.
output_examples.output: A focused CI plan that uses affected tasks, a full git history checkout, controlled parallelism, and remote cache guidance.

output_examples.input: Developers keep importing feature code from unrelated domains.
output_examples.output: A dependency policy based on type and scope tags, with examples of allowed and blocked relationships.

best_practices: Use clear type and scope tags before enforcing module boundaries.
Keep real cache tokens in CI secrets, not committed configuration files.
Start with affected lint, test, and build tasks before adding complex workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not copy token placeholders into versioned Nx configuration with real values.
Do not enforce boundaries before project tags are consistent.
Do not split libraries so finely that simple changes touch many projects.

faq.question: Does this skill run Nx commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and command examples for an agent or developer to apply deliberately.

faq.question: Can it help migrate an existing monorepo to Nx?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan workspace structure, tags, project configuration, and affected command adoption.

faq.question: Does it support Nx Cloud?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes remote caching guidance, but real tokens should stay in secrets management.

faq.question: What projects does it fit best?
faq.answer: It fits JavaScript and TypeScript monorepos that use Nx for apps, libraries, CI, and generators.

faq.question: Can it enforce architectural rules?
faq.answer: It can propose module boundary rules using Nx tags. Enforcement still depends on project configuration.

faq.question: Does it replace Nx documentation?
faq.answer: No. It gives reusable patterns and should be checked against the current Nx version.
